Type: We Are Young Money is the first compilation album by American hip hop record label Young Money Entertainment, and released on December 21, 2009 by Young Money Entertainment, Cash Money Records and Universal Motown Records. The album features contributions from Young Money artists Lil Wayne, Gudda Gudda, Jae Millz, Mack Maine, Tyga, T-Streets, Short Dawg, Shanell, Nicki Minaj, Lil Twist, Lil Chuckee, and of course, Drake. The album also features guest appearances from Birdman, Lloyd and Gucci Mane.

Type: Thank Me Later is the debut studio album by Canadian rapper and singer Drake. It was released on June 15, 2010. Production for the album took place at various recording studios during 2009 to 2010 and was mostly produced by longtime collaborators 40 and Boi-1da. It also featured contributions from Timbaland, Swizz Beatz, Nicki Minaj, Lil Wayne, The-Dream, and Kanye West, among others.

Type: It's Never Enough was a R&B mixtape that Drake was planning to release in 2011, but it was scrapped in favor of Take Care. The only single released for this project is "I Get Lonely Too", which is a remix of TLC's "Fanmail". The cover for this era is a recreation of the alleged cover art for the mixtape, originally found on official merchandise from Drake.

Type: Take Care is the second studio album by Canadian rapper Drake. It was released on November 15, 2011. The album features guest appearances from the Weeknd, Rihanna, Kendrick Lamar, Birdman, Nicki Minaj, Rick Ross, Lil Wayne, and André 3000. Alongside prominent production from the album's executive producers Drake and 40, further contributors include T-Minus, Chantal Kreviazuk, Boi-1da, Illangelo, Jamie xx, Supa Dups, Just Blaze, Chase N. Cashe, and Doc McKinney.

Type: Views, originally called Views from the 6, is the fourth studio album from Drake, and his 10th overall project. It was released on April 29, 2016, by Young Money Entertainment, Cash Money Records, and Republic Records. The album's title was first announced as Views from the 6, as it first appeared from a report in a July 2014 article from Billboard. According to Drake on Twitter, "the 6" is a reference to his hometown of Toronto, Ontario. On April 29, 2016, it was revealed that the title had been shortened to Views.

Type: The original version of Drake's sixth studio album "Certified Lover Boy". Production for the album first started sometime in 2018, but production wouldn't actually get going until sometime in September 2019. This early version of the album had a much darker, moodier tone, blending introspective lyrics with lo-fi and R&B influences. It differed greatly from the polished, radio-friendly version released in 2021. Most of the material from this version of Certified Lover Boy would be released officially on Drake's 2020 mixtape, Dark Lane Demo Tapes.

Type: In late 2020, Drake tore his ACL and underwent surgery, leading to the delay of his sixth studio album, Certified Lover Boy, originally planned to release January 2021. The injury and rehab shifted his focus away from music, forcing him to postpone the release. During recovery, Drake continued refining the album, resulting in a more introspective and emotionally layered project. The extra time allowed for production tweaks but also caused some creative indecision. CLB was ultimately released on September 3, 2021, with a major rollout, marking a shift in tone shaped in part by his physical and mental recovery process.

Type: Her Loss is a collaborative album by Toronto rapper Drake and Atlanta rapper 21 Savage. The album was initially slated for release on October 28, 2022, but was pushed back one week to November 4, 2022, following Noah “40” Shebib’s COVID-19 diagnosis. The album features a sole guest appearance from Travis Scott. It is the third part of what Drake described as "a trilogy of albums", following Certified Lover Boy (2021) and Honestly, Nevermind (2022).

Name (Check out the tracker website!):
100 GIGS
(100 GIGS FOR YOUR HEADTOP, plottttwisttttt)
℗ 2024 OVO under executive license to Republic Records, a division of UMG Recordings
Notes (Join the Discord for updates and:
(07/04/2024) (Drake uploads unreleased content on his then-private website 100gigs.org)
(08/06/2024) (Drake makes 100gigs.org public)
(08/23/2024) (Drake releases the seemingly final update of 100gigs.org)
Type: On August 6, 2024, the Instagram account plottttwistttttt, rumored to be a Drake burner account, posted three new songs and linked to a little-known website called 100 Gigs For Your Headtop. This site featured embedded versions and download links for the tracks, along with unreleased content and behind-the-scenes pictures/videos, dating back to July 4. The songs were quickly removed from Instagram and the download option was disabled, but the media remained accessible. On August 10, the three-track single was officially released on streaming services. On August 30, the single was re-released with "Blue Green Red" replaced by "Circadian Rhythm," while the original version stayed available.

Name (Check out the tracker website!): ICEMAN [V1]
Notes (Join the Discord for updates and:
(06/28/2024) (Drake teases Iceman for the first time on the plottttwistttttt Instagram account)
(02/14/2025) ($ome $exy $ongs 4 U officially releases)
(05/24/2025) (Drake confirms the Iceman name in an Instagram caption and post)
(07/05/2025) (What Did I Miss? releases as the first single for ICEMAN [V1])
(10/10/2025) (Drake reworks ICEMAN)
Type: Following the release of 100 GIGS, Drake began teasing his ninth studio album, hinting at the name ICEMAN through vague Instagram posts that included the X-Men character, F1 racer Kimi Raikkonen (AKA The Iceman) and a variety of ice sculptures. The ICEMAN title was confirmed in July 2025 as Drake officially started the rollout for the album with the single 'WHAT DID I MISS' and  the beginning of an episodic series where Drake would drop singles, snippets and give clues and hints about the subject matter of the album. The cover for this era was used as a placeholder on DSPs for ICEMAN, similar to What Did I Miss?.

Name (Check out the tracker website!):
ICEMAN [V2]
(MAID OF HONOUR, HABIBTI)
Notes (Join the Discord for updates and:
(10/10/2025) (Drake reworks ICEMAN)
(05/15/2026) (ICEMAN, MAID OF HONOUR & HABIBTI officially release)
Type: On October 10th, 2025 (which was initially the release date for ICEMAN, although unannounced), Drake decided to rework the album, seemingly last minute. He continued teasing the album throughout early 2026 with cryptic posts and appearances, including iced-out courtside seats at Toronto Raptors games. On April 19th, 2026, an ice structure appeared in Toronto, with a bag containing a booklet with concept art from Ben Dorado for the album alongside the album’s release date that was inside of the structure. A streamer named Kishka managed to access the bag by breaking off ice blocks and got asked to come outside of Drake’s crib to officially release the booklet’s contents and the album’s release date on April 21st, 2026. Morgan Wallen, Future, Young Thug & Sexyy Red are allegedly on the album. The cover for this era is from icemancountdown.com page 63, which, although official, may not be the cover for the album as no cover has been confirmed as of yet.
